layout.staging_payload: optional sole child named payload; same-filesystem single-link regular file no larger than generation_bytes_max

canonical_json.encoding: UTF-8 without BOM, whitespace, or trailing newline
canonical_json.object_members: closed and sorted by Unicode code point
canonical_json.arrays: preserve declared semantic order unless a record explicitly declares sorting
canonical_json.strings: preserve raw Unicode scalar values without normalization; emit raw UTF-8 except JSON-required quote, reverse-solidus, and control escapes
canonical_json.u64: minimal unsigned decimal string; zero is exactly 0 and no leading zero is allowed
canonical_json.u32: JSON integer from 0 through 4294967295 with no fraction or exponent
canonical_json.f32: exact IEEE-754 bits as exactly eight lowercase hexadecimal characters
canonical_json.f64: exact IEEE-754 bits as exactly sixteen lowercase hexadecimal characters
canonical_json.numbers_forbidden: all JSON numbers except fields declared u32
canonical_json.non_finite: rejected by the owning domain type before encoding and after decoding

digests.exact_object: SHA-256 over exact stored bytes with no domain prefix; typed form sha256:<64-lowercase-hex>
digests.generation_preimage: domain || canonical-generation-byte-length as big-endian u64 || canonical-generation-bytes
digests.path_digest: the bare 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters from the applicable digest

ref.header: bytes 0..7 magic; 8..9 schema-version big-endian u16; 10 kind u8; 11 presence u8; 12..15 total-length big-endian u32
ref.project_uuid: bytes 16..31 in UUID network byte order
ref.current_digest: bytes 32..63 raw generation digest; always present
ref.previous_digest: bytes 64..95 raw generation digest or all zero when absent
ref.base_digest: bytes 96..127 raw manual-generation digest or all zero when absent
ref.checksum: bytes 128..159 raw SHA-256 defined by digests.ref_checksum_preimage
ref.path_kind_binding: refs/head=manual_head, refs/recovery=manual_recovery, refs/autosave-head=autosave_head, refs/autosave-recovery=autosave_recovery, and refs/pins/<checkpoint-id>=pin; swapping kinds is corruption
ref.unused_slots: every slot forbidden or absent for its kind is all zero; presence bits and the allowed per-kind slot combination must agree exactly

kinds.name: autosave_head
kinds.base: optional only for a provisional project; otherwise required

store_states.established: refs/head is present; refs/autosave-head, when present, has a required base equal to its current generation's base_manual_generation_id; comparison with refs/head.current classifies it as same-base or divergent
store_states.provisional: refs/head and refs/recovery are absent; refs/autosave-head is present with base absent, and its current autosave generation has base_manual_generation_id=null; every other combination without refs/head is corruption

ref.recovery_pair_rule: independently for the manual and autosave lanes: without a head, recovery is forbidden; with a head, recovery may be absent only when head.previous is absent, recovery.current may equal present head.previous for a clean pair, or recovery.current may equal head.current for an interrupted pre-head commit; the existing head.current remains authoritative and no ref is repaired automatically; any other recovery target is corruption

generation.generation_sequence: u64; initial zero and otherwise one greater than the maximum sequence in the current manual and autosave heads observed under the writer lease; diagnostic ordering only, never authority
generation.revision_high_water_sequence: u64 and not less than revision_sequence
generation.parent_generation_id: same-lane generation ID or null; provenance only and never a GC liveness edge
generation.base_manual_generation_id: required for autosave after a manual save, null for manual and provisional autosave
generation.forked_from: null or closed {project_id,generation_id}; fixed for the project and repeated unchanged in every generation created after Save As
generation.artifacts: the ProjectState artifact records in semantic order; every record contains one logical_object and one closed storage binding
generation.reachable_objects: the exact physical object closure derived from all artifact storage bindings, sorted by digest then byte length; duplicate digests are forbidden and no extra object is permitted

state_projection.optional_fields: dataset locator_hint/package_id/release_id and artifact derivation_id/recipe_id are always present and encode absence as JSON null

tagged_variant_shapes.render_dvr: closed {density_scale,mode,opacity_transfer,sampling} with mode=dvr; opacity_transfer is closed {curve,window}

state_projection.quaternion_restore: UnitQuaternion::from_canonical_xyzw preserves accepted f64 bits exactly

logical_object_binding.page_field_types: ordinal is u32; offset and byte_length are u64; digest is ExactBytesDigest
logical_object_binding.page_rules: pages are nonempty and sorted by ordinal; ordinal starts at zero, offset starts at zero, offsets are contiguous, every non-final page is exactly 16777216 bytes, the final page is 1 through 16777216 bytes, and concatenated length/digest equal logical_descriptor

direct.rule: object digest and byte length equal the artifact logical_object descriptor

paged.rule: media type and role equal this record's fixed logical-object-binding values

payload_storage.direct: a logical object of at most 16777216 bytes is stored once at its own ExactBytesDigest and its stored descriptor exactly equals the logical descriptor
payload_storage.paged: a logical object above 16777216 bytes is split at deterministic 16777216-byte offsets; pages and one canonical logical-object-binding manifest are stored as exact objects
payload_storage.paged_validation: page ordinals and offsets are contiguous from zero, every non-final page is exactly 16777216 bytes, page digests validate, concatenated bytes match the logical length and digest, and manifest logical facts equal the ProjectState RawObjectDescriptor
payload_storage.logical_binding_in_generation: each artifact record binds its logical descriptor to direct or paged storage; reachable_objects separately lists exactly every direct object or binding manifest and page object needed by those bindings

public_api_contract.actor: starts unbound, then owns at most one live normal or recovery-only session, every OS lease, the bounded request/completion queues, and the sole active filesystem transaction
public_api_contract.session: immutable safe metadata only: project ID, current manual/autosave generation IDs, effective open mode, and a display-safe package root; no lock or internal object paths
public_api_contract.writable_fallback: only prefer_writable may become an explicitly reported read_only session when the writer lease is unavailable or the held existing-store filesystem tuple is unqualified; read_only remains read_only, while Create, SaveAs, first provisional Autosave, and every other new destination on an unqualified tuple fail with unsupported_filesystem before source reads or mutation
public_api_contract.commit_capture: immutable validated ProjectGenerationProjection plus expected lane parent, autosave base when applicable, fork provenance for Save As, and bounded lazy object sources
public_api_contract.object_source: opaque cancellable stream factory bound to one expected RawObjectDescriptor; no PathBuf, unbounded owned bytes, or preopened object collection
public_api_contract.save_as: caller supplies a projection with the new ProjectId and explicit source generation; the actor changes session only after durable destination installation and retains the old session on failure
public_api_contract.completion: every accepted request produces exactly one terminal completion carrying its ProjectStoreRequestId, including cancellation and an autosave superseded by coalescing; successful Opened and RecoveryOpened completions carry (ProjectStoreSession, ProjectGenerationProjection) in that order, while Created remains session-only because its caller supplied the created projection
public_api_contract.receipt: opaque operation-specific anti-TOCTOU token; durable commits carry captured revision/high-water and new/current/previous generation IDs; established autosave receipts carry their required base manual generation, provisional autosave receipts carry no base, and neither can mark a project saved
public_api_contract.recovery_candidate: bounded metadata only with generation, captured revision, origin, base/current relation, recovery classification, and recoverability summary; inspection may decode projection state internally for bounded validation but never returns or exposes it, and only successful explicit OpenRecovery returns the selected projection

recovery_classification.provisional: the candidate is an autosave, no manual head exists, and its base is absent
recovery_classification.newer: the candidate is an autosave, its base equals the current manual generation, and its revision_sequence differs from the current manual revision_sequence; revision IDs are immutable state identities, so numeric revision order is irrelevant
recovery_classification.stale: the candidate is an autosave, its base equals the current manual generation, and its revision_sequence equals the current manual revision_sequence
recovery_classification.divergent: the candidate is an autosave outside the provisional and same-base cases
recovery_classification.manual_branch: the candidate is a manual previous, manual recovery, or manual generation found by bounded orphan scan

public_api_contract.recovery_selection: every explicitly selected recovery projection opens dirty as an unsaved branch; orphan autosaves retain their autosave relation classification, session head IDs remain actual ref facts, and selection never repairs, promotes, or rewrites a ref
public_api_contract.diagnostics: bounded counters and stable reason codes only; no absolute/internal paths or raw OS error strings
public_api_contract.cancellation: cancelled means current work stopped at a safe boundary; existing non-maintenance operations change no authority; Trash or Purge may leave current-invocation maintenance progress only as a fully synced retryable batch prefix, while cancellation before maintenance progress leaves any preexisting state unchanged
public_api_contract.config: contains no filesystem-qualification bypass, hard-limit bypass, retry, compatibility, or alternate-path switch

public_actor_lifecycle_contract.methods: ProjectStoreActor::start(config), nonblocking try_submit, nonblocking try_recv, has_exited, and joined cancellation shutdown through join; Drop requests cancellation and does not block
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.ownership: one worker is unbound or owns exactly one held root and its leases; filesystem work never runs on the caller thread and an installed package crosses directly into actor ownership without close/reopen
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.autosave_destination: Autosave carries destination as Option<ProjectStorePath>; unbound first provisional publication requires Some, while bound provisional or established advance requires None; every other combination fails before mutation
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.create: unbound Create installs a fresh initial manual package; Create from a healthy provisional session performs a same-project, same-science, fork-absent, non-regressing-high-water initial-manual handoff to an absent destination and transfers ownership only after durable success
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.old_unsaved_package: a successful first-manual handoff does not mutate, delete, repair, or retain authority over the old provisional package; bounded application recovery-area pruning remains later work
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.open: unbound Open honors the requested mode and returns the actual held session plus authoritative projection for a healthy established or provisional store; failure before bounded recovery validation leaves the actor unbound
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.recovery_only: when bounded recovery validation succeeds but normal authority loading fails, Open returns the normal fault while retaining a recovery-only session; it accepts InspectRecovery, OpenRecovery, FullVerify, Cancel, and Close, rejects every mutation, and after successful explicit selection additionally permits only SaveAs with that exact selected generation, a new ProjectId, and fork provenance as a new authority
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.normal_bound: normal sessions retain the accepted session commands; ManualSave is established-only, a provisional first manual project uses Create, and a recovery-selected project uses only the corrected SaveAs flow
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.binding_conflict: Create or Open against an incompatible bound session completes with a typed corruption fault and changes no session
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.completion: every accepted request still produces exactly one existing terminal correlated completion; first provisional publication uses Autosaved and no new completion or fault variant is added
public_actor_lifecycle_contract.close: Close is valid unbound or bound, cancels queued and active work at accepted safe boundaries, releases the held session, emits Closed, and terminates the worker; joined shutdown creates no new completion obligation

provisional_autosave_contract.base: an established-project autosave requires the current manual generation as its base; a provisional autosave has no manual base in its capture, generation, head ref, or receipt
provisional_autosave_contract.destination: the first private provisional publication takes one caller-supplied validated ProjectStorePath and never chooses the application recovery directory or invents another ProjectId
provisional_autosave_contract.first_capture: expected_parent, autosave_base, and forked_from are absent; the projection ProjectId equals the envelope ProjectId
provisional_autosave_contract.first_publication: create autosave generation sequence zero with no base, publish its complete immutable closure and generation, publish only a base-less refs/autosave-head, validate and sync the sibling package, install it by no-clobber rename, and sync the destination parent
provisional_autosave_contract.advance: require expected_parent equal to the current autosave head, no autosave base, matching project/scientific identity and fork facts, and non-regressing revision high-water; use the autosave recovery-before-head protocol and remain provisional
provisional_autosave_contract.retry: never overwrite; a fresh retry may adopt only an exact fully validated destination created by the same first capture and completes the required destination-parent sync; every unrelated or mismatched destination fails closed
provisional_autosave_contract.cancellation: first-publication cancellation before package install removes only the owned sibling stage and changes no destination; during advance, cancellation before recovery-ref publication may leave validated immutable orphans, while cancellation after durable recovery-ref publication may leave only the accepted recovery-ahead state with the old autosave head authoritative; defer cancellation across package install and each ref replacement through required syncs; post-authority uncertainty is commit_indeterminate and requires fresh reopen/retry
provisional_autosave_contract.transitions: first publication composes the existing commit_prefix, object, and generation sets, the autosave-parameterized head_ref set, package_stage_create, package_tree_sync, package_install_noreplace, and destination_parent_sync; advance composes the existing commit_prefix, object, and generation sets plus the autosave-parameterized recovery_ref and head_ref sets
provisional_autosave_contract.timers: B2 publishes and coalesces explicitly submitted captures only; B3 application integration owns the 30-second idle and 120-second maximum scheduling because application owns live revision and capture creation; exact trigger, reset, and monotonic-clock semantics remain unfrozen and this correction authorizes no timer implementation

staging_cleanup_contract.trigger: after existing-store open acquires the writer lease, completes bounded read-side store/recovery validation, and reconfirms the writer against the same held root immediately before removal; cleanup finishes before writable use or another mutation; read-only requests and writer-contended read-only fallbacks never clean staging; shared maintenance remains sufficient
staging_cleanup_contract.selection: every direct staging child must match the exact transaction grammar; PID is syntax only and process liveness, age, hostname, and lock text are never consulted
staging_cleanup_contract.contents: staging, each transaction directory, and each payload stay on the held store root's filesystem; a transaction is empty or contains only one single-link regular payload no larger than generation_bytes_max; payload bytes are not hashed, decoded, or interpreted
staging_cleanup_contract.preflight: completely and boundedly validate every staging child and record its identity before the first removal; one unknown, malformed, linked, cross-device, oversized, changed, or over-cap entry rejects the whole cleanup without mutation
staging_cleanup_contract.order: process transaction IDs in unsigned bytewise order; revalidate identities; remove payload if present and sync that transaction directory; remove the empty transaction directory and sync top-level staging; when staging exists but is already empty, sync it once; never create missing staging; retain staging itself and finally prove every existing staging directory empty
staging_cleanup_contract.transitions: compose staging_cleanup_payload_remove, staging_cleanup_directory_sync, and staging_cleanup_transaction_remove; staging_cleanup_directory_sync repeats for transaction and top-level staging directories and uses before/after plus zero-based occurrence injection
staging_cleanup_contract.cancellation: observe cancellation only during complete preflight, where it changes no bytes; after preflight defer cancellation through the full ordered remove, sync, and re-inventory sweep
staging_cleanup_contract.indeterminate: any required sync failure or any failure after the first successful removal returns commit_indeterminate, suspends writes, performs no rollback, and requires close/reopen plus fresh retry
staging_cleanup_contract.retry: fresh retry accepts a now-empty transaction or an already-absent transaction, re-preflights the visible namespace, syncs an existing empty staging directory even with zero removals, never creates missing staging, is idempotent, and introduces no marker or ownership record
staging_cleanup_contract.scope: touch only canonical direct children of internal staging; never remove sibling package stages, published immutable orphans, refs, locks, trash, generations, objects, or staging itself
staging_cleanup_contract.claim_boundary: focused callback and process-crash evidence does not prove hostile-writer safety, power-loss durability, or filesystem qualification

transition_inventory.injection: each named transition supports before and after injection plus a zero-based occurrence for every repeated transition
transition_inventory.commit_indeterminate: failure to establish final ref-directory durability after retry suspends writes, keeps the revision dirty, and requires close/reopen
transition_inventory.saved_revision_rule: only a durable manual-head completion may advance exactly its captured revision; autosave never advances saved_revision

filesystem_tuple.selection: use the held root descriptor's statx mount ID to select exactly one /proc/self/mountinfo record and require the exact normalized filesystem, magic, VFS-option set, and super-option set; missing, ambiguous, unreadable, unparsable, or different facts are unqualified
filesystem_tuple.probe_rule: runtime capability probes never add a qualified tuple

runtime.existing_prefer_writable: an unqualified tuple returns an explicitly reported read_only session without writer-only cleanup or mutation
runtime.new_destination: Create, SaveAs, first provisional Autosave, and every other new destination on an unqualified tuple fail with unsupported_filesystem before source reads, staging, or destination mutation
runtime.bypass: no production feature, configuration, environment variable, alternate path, or runtime probe bypass; a crate-private cfg(test)-only detector seam is permitted

durability_qualification_contract.hosted_coverage: every named transition supports deterministic before and after injection, lane separation, and zero-based selection of every repeated occurrence
durability_qualification_contract.process_kill_coverage: fresh-process SIGKILL, reopen, and exact retry cover every occurrence that mutates filesystem or lease state or can leave writer-private residue; pure reads, validation, comparison, root scans, and candidate listing require deterministic injection and byte-identical no-mutation evidence instead

vm_coverage.rule: cut one pre-sequence baseline and each distinct post-transition persistence or authority boundary below; do not duplicate an adjacent equivalent before state, but keep distinct lanes, directories, and repeated mutation states
vm_coverage.hosted_only: Open, commit-prefix comparison, private stage creation/write/copy, staging cleanup, maintenance lease upgrade/restore, root scan, and candidate listing receive no VM cuts

harness.cross_device: positive copy-and-rehash Save As uses the two independent ext4 filesystems
harness.power_loss: rootless nbdkit cache=writeback discards guest-unpersisted writes while explicit guest flushes reach the backing image

durability_qualification_contract.report: bind commit/tree/clean state, fixture and executable digests, exact qualification tuple, filesystem features, filesystem/kernel/mount/QEMU/nbdkit facts, hosted/process/VM transition and kill matrices, byte/object/work counters, product display facts, source digests, performance facts, results, and every failure without private paths
durability_qualification_contract.claim_boundary: these are accepted B2 requirements, not a claim that the gate, lane, hostile fixture, VM harness, measurements, or evidence currently exists or passes

trash_contract.selection: nonempty duplicate-free generation IDs bounded by recovery_candidates_max; fresh exclusive preflight proves each active selection is orphaned, while an absent active generation is accepted only as an exact already-quarantined retry; PlanCompaction never authorizes mutation
trash_contract.non_regenerable: every selected generation declares zero non-regenerable artifacts; otherwise reject before mutation with confirmation_required, which the current API cannot satisfy
trash_contract.retained_closure: move selected generation files and union(selected physical-object closures) minus union(all active unselected generation closures); roots and unselected orphans are retained, shared objects stay active, and anonymous unrooted objects are never swept
trash_contract.maintenance: require a confirmed writer and cancellably convert the actor's own shared maintenance descriptor to exclusive; restore shared ownership immediately after a failed conversion and on every exit; losing maintenance ownership suspends the session and fails indeterminately
trash_contract.preflight: before mutation, completely and boundedly validate active and trash namespaces, single-link regular types, canonical generations, refs/pins/graph, declared object lengths, selection, capacity/fan-out, destinations, and the deterministic move plan; unknown or mismatched entries fail closed
trash_contract.order_and_batches: generation IDs first, then object digests; each durable batch is at most gc_batch_entries_max namespace mutations and gc_batch_bytes_max checked source bytes, with every affected active/trash parent directory synced deterministically
trash_contract.retry: never overwrite; active exact plus trash absent is a no-replace rename, active absent plus trash exact is already quarantined, both exact with an unretained active file syncs trash before removing the active duplicate, every mismatch or double absence fails closed, and a fully quarantined selection succeeds with zero work
trash_contract.cancellation: lease wait, preflight, and pre-batch boundaries are cancellable; from a batch's first directory creation, rename, or duplicate removal through its final required directory sync cancellation is deferred; a between-batch cancellation may leave only a durable idempotent prefix, so cancelled means a safe boundary rather than necessarily zero maintenance progress
trash_contract.indeterminate: any non-cancellation failure after the first mutation returns commit_indeterminate, suspends writes, performs no rollback, and requires close/reopen; pre-mutation failure changes no bytes
trash_contract.diagnostics: published_objects counts files newly removed from active and durably present in trash; streamed_bytes counts only exact-collision verification bytes; neither is a reclaim estimate
trash_contract.future_non_regenerable: Trash or Purge of non-regenerable artifacts requires a separate API correction with opaque snapshot-bound exact artifact authorization and verified-backup proof; a boolean is forbidden

purge_contract.selection: the complete freshly inventoried canonical trash snapshot; an empty snapshot is successful zero-namespace-mutation work, and there is no age, caller-selected, automatic, or active-store deletion mode
purge_contract.authorization: every remaining trash generation must be canonical, project-consistent, and declare zero non-regenerable artifacts; otherwise reject before unlink with confirmation_required, which the current API cannot satisfy
purge_contract.maintenance: require a confirmed writer and cancellably convert the actor's own shared maintenance descriptor to exclusive; preflight and deletion occur only while exclusive, every exit restores shared ownership, and maintenance loss suspends the session and fails indeterminately
purge_contract.preflight: completely and boundedly validate the active graph and active-plus-trash namespaces, canonical single-link regular generation and object files, project identity, closures, lengths, exact present trash-object digests, fan-out, and capacity; missing generation parent or base IDs remain permitted provenance rather than liveness edges; unknown, malformed, foreign, linked, mismatched, or unreferenced trash content fails closed
purge_contract.incomplete_trash: every trash generation is absent active; an active-only closure object is allowed only when retained by an active generation, while an unretained active-only closure object returns source_changed so Trash finishes first; every present trash object is referenced by an accepted trash generation; an exact active-plus-trash duplicate deletes only the trash file when an active generation retains the active copy, otherwise it returns source_changed
purge_contract.order_and_barrier: delete trash objects first in digest order while retaining all trash generations as authorization; sync affected object directories, sync every existing object fan-out including empty, re-inventory, and prove the object namespace empty before deleting remaining generations in generation-ID order; keep the bounded directory hierarchy
purge_contract.retry: while all authorizing generations remain, exclude only active-only closure objects retained by active generations; absent trash-owned objects are already purged only when they form an exact digest-ordered prefix followed by the present suffix, and every other absence pattern fails closed; this exact-prefix rule applies only to objects; after the synced empty-object barrier, independently revalidate remaining generations and permit absent provenance parents or bases without claiming a runtime-verifiable generation prefix
purge_contract.preunlink_identity: hold the validated file and no-follow parent descriptors and immediately before unlink revalidate regular single-link type, device and inode, length, and expected generation identity or object digest against the held fact; mismatch fails closed; exclusive maintenance excludes compliant writers, while hostile external replacement in the final Linux check-to-unlink race is outside the supported writer model
purge_contract.batches: each durable batch has at most gc_batch_entries_max descriptor-relative no-follow unlinks and gc_batch_bytes_max validated file bytes; lease wait and initial inventory may cancel before maintenance progress; once a retry prefix is recognized, a recovery-sync sweep begins, or a batch unlinks, cancellation is deferred through the complete sync and re-inventory boundary; only current-invocation progress may be returned as a synced idempotent prefix
purge_contract.indeterminate: any required recovery-sync failure or non-cancellation failure after the first successful unlink returns commit_indeterminate, suspends writes, performs no rollback, and requires close/reopen; a pre-unlink failure changes no namespace bytes
purge_contract.diagnostics: published_objects counts regular files newly removed from trash by this invocation, including generation JSON and object files; streamed_bytes counts exact bytes read from those present trash files for identity and integrity validation; neither is a reclaim estimate, and a zero-removal retry reports zero published_objects
purge_contract.transitions: compose gc_maintenance_upgrade and gc_maintenance_restore with purge_remove and purge_directory_sync; every observed occurrence supports before and after injection plus fresh-process kill, reopen, and retry
purge_contract.claim_boundary: callback and SIGKILL evidence is logic and process-crash evidence only, not power-loss simulation, filesystem qualification, or durability

fixture_manifest.required_cases: established newer autosave, stale same-base autosave, divergent autosave, provisional autosave-only project, exact manual/autosave recovery-to-previous pairing, positive recovery-ahead with and without head.previous, and unrelated same-lane recovery rejection
